perturbation.original_text: Clause 1 Confidential Information - information of whatever kind and in whatever form contained (and includes in particular but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, documents, drawings, computerized information, films, tapes, specifications, designs, models, equipment or data of any kind) which is clearly identified by the Disclosing Party as confidential by an appropriate legend or if orally disclosed then upon disclosure or within 30 days of such oral disclosure identified in writing by the Disclosing Party as confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 1 Confidential Information - information of whatever kind and in whatever form contained (and includes in particular but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, documents, drawings, computerized information, films, tapes, specifications, designs, models, equipment or data of any kind) which is identified as confidential, regardless of whether it's marked by the Disclosing Party. If orally disclosed then it is deemed confidential with no requirement of written confirmation by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' requires clear identification by the Disclosing Party, either through a legend or written confirmation after oral disclosure. The modified version removes the requirement of marking the information as confidential and eliminates the need for written confirmation for orally disclosed information. This creates an **in-text contradiction** with Clause 2, which outlines the Receiving Party's obligations concerning 'Confidential Information' as originally defined, since they are no longer aligned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Clause 1 alters the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing the requirement for the Disclosing Party to explicitly identify information as confidential, either through marking or written confirmation. This directly contradicts Clause 2, which outlines the Receiving Party's obligations specifically concerning 'Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party'. Since the definition has been altered to include information not actively identified as confidential by the disclosing party, it creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential unless the Disclosing Party explicitly states otherwise. The Receiving party is entitled to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party; b) that it shall make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, for any purpose; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to any person; d) that it shall copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, to the extent necessary for any purposes; and e) that it shall not keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Clause 2 directly contradicts the original intent of the NDA. Originally, the Receiving Party was obligated to treat Confidential Information as strictly confidential, requiring written consent for disclosure, using it only for LOA purposes, and restricting access and reproduction. The modified clause now states that the Receiving Party can disclose the information freely, use it for any purpose, grant access to anyone, reproduce it as needed, and not keep records. This is a direct **in-text contradiction** with Clause 1, which aims to define and protect confidential information. This change voids the intended protection of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 1 Confidential Information - information of whatever kind and in whatever form contained (and includes in particular but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, documents, drawings, computerized information, films, tapes, specifications, designs, models, equipment or data of any kind) which is clearly identified by the Disclosing Party as confidential by an appropriate legend or if orally disclosed then upon disclosure or within 30 days of such oral disclosure identified in writing by the Disclosing Party as confidential. Disclosing Party - the party from time to time disclosing Confidential Information. 1 Receiving Party - the party from time to time receiving Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Clause 2 directly contradicts the original intent and effect of Clause 1, which defines and protects confidential information. The original text in Clause 2 imposed strict restrictions on the Receiving Party regarding the use, disclosure, access, and reproduction of Confidential Information. The changed version removes those restrictions, allowing the Receiving Party to disclose, use, and reproduce the information freely. This directly conflicts with Clause 1, which aims to protect confidential information as identified by the Disclosing Party, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 5 Either Party shall, upon 30 days written request by the other Party, return to the other party all Confidential Information which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof together with a confirmation in writing that the Confidential Information in computerized form has been destroyed. Furthermore, at the request of the Disclosing Party the Receiving Party shall immediately cease to use the Confidential Information for any purposes whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 5 Either Party may, upon their own discretion, return to the other party all Confidential Information which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof, if they wish to do so. No confirmation is necessary. Furthermore, the Receiving Party can keep using the Confidential Information for any purposes whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the return of Confidential Information upon request, with written confirmation of destruction of computerized copies, and immediate cessation of use. The modified clause makes returning the information optional, eliminates the need for confirmation, and allows continued use of the information. This is a direct **in-text contradiction** with Clause 2, which aims to limit how the receiving party can use the disclosed information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Clause 5 directly contradicts Clause 2. Clause 2 explicitly restricts the Receiving Party's use of Confidential Information to 'LOA purposes' and mandates strict confidentiality. The modified Clause 5, however, allows the Receiving Party to use the Confidential Information for 'any purposes whatsoever,' which is a direct violation of the restrictions in Clause 2. Additionally, Clause 2(d) restricts copying or reproducing confidential information to the extent necessary for LOA purposes, whereas changed Clause 5 implies no restrictions on usage and therefore no restrictions on copying and reproducing, thereby contradicting Clause 2 (d). Since the changed text breaks direct compatibility with the contradicted section, we can assign 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 6 The NDA may also involve the exchange of classified information (which may or may not be Confidential Information), the handling of which shall be treated by the Parties according to applicable laws and regulations and other administrative acts and policies, now or hereinafter in effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 6 The NDA involves the exchange of only public available information, the handling of which shall be treated by the Parties without any laws and regulations or other administrative acts and policies.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause acknowledges that the NDA may involve classified information subject to applicable laws and regulations. The modification restricts the exchange to only public available information, and says that those will not be governed by any laws or regulations. This change directly **contradicts** the introduction which states "noting that such exchange of information may involve the disclosure of confidential information by either Party, and that the Parties wish to record their agreement to maintain the confidentiality of such information". If only public information is going to be shared, then there is no need for an NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Introduction This Non Disclosure Agreement (the "NDA") is made by and between the Ministry of Defence of the Kingdom of Norway ("NMOD") and Gripen International AB ("GI"), a business unit of Saab AB (publ), hereinafter jointly referred to as the "Parties" and individually referred to as "Party". The Parties: are referring to the letter of agreement between NMOD and GI concerning industrial participation in further development of Gripen and related projects (the "LOA"); noting their wish to exchange information for the purpose of conducting evaluations of the Gripen aircraft, and pursuing discussions and work related to the Gripen Demonstrator Programme and other Gripen and defence related projects ("LOA Projects"); noting that such exchange of information may involve the disclosure of confidential information by either Party, and that the Parties wish to record their agreement to maintain the confidentiality of such information; noting that exchanging information with GI shall also include exchanging information with Saab and upon such disclosure Saab shall confirm and be bound of the terms and conditions contained herein as if Saab was a Party to the NDA; noting that exchanging information with the NMOD shall also include other Norwegian authorities and upon such disclosure said Norwegian authority shall confirm and be bound of the terms and conditions contained herein as if said Norwegian authority was a Party to the NDA.
perturbation.justification: The original clause 6 acknowledges the NDA may involve classified information, while the changed clause 6 limits the NDA to only publicly available information and removes the need for applicable laws. This directly contradicts the Introduction, which states that the exchange of information may involve confidential information and outlines the need to maintain its confidentiality. The change creates a strong contradiction because if only public information is exchanged, an NDA is unnecessary, rendering the introductory clauses regarding confidentiality agreements meaningless. This meets the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 12 The NDA shall become effective on the date on the date of signatures and shall be valid for a period of two years thereafter, when it shall automatically expire 4 unless otherwise agreed in writing between the parties. However, all Confidential Information exchanged during the validity hereof shall be subject to the obligations and restrictions under the NDA (including the undertakings in Clause 13 and 14 below) for a period of five years following expiration or other termination of the NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 12 The NDA shall become effective on the date on the date of signatures and shall be valid in perpetuity, unless either party decides unilaterally to terminate it at any time. Confidential Information exchanged during the validity hereof will not be subject to any restrictions following termination of the NDA.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause sets a two-year validity period with a five-year restriction on Confidential Information post-expiration. The modified clause makes the agreement perpetual unless terminated, and removes all restrictions on Confidential Information after termination. This creates an **in-text contradiction** with all previous clauses that establish obligations of confidentiality, thus removing any actual constraints on information usage following termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a two-year validity period for the NDA with a five-year restriction on Confidential Information post-expiration. The changed text makes the NDA perpetual unless terminated, and removes all restrictions on Confidential Information after termination. This directly contradicts Clause 2, which outlines the Receiving Party's obligations to treat Confidential Information as strictly confidential and restricts its use, copying, and disclosure, because the changed text explicitly states that these restrictions do not apply after termination. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 13 The NDA shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws and regulations of Norway.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 13 The NDA shall not be governed by any laws or regulations.
perturbation.explanation: The original Clause states that the NDA is governed by Norwegian laws and regulations. The modified clause explicitly states that the NDA is not governed by any laws or regulations. This **contradicts** the implicit assumption throughout the entire document that the NDA operates within a legal framework, potentially rendering the NDA unenforceable, especially when referencing other authorities such as NMOD which is a public Norwegian Authority.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Introduction This Non Disclosure Agreement (the "NDA") is made by and between the Ministry of Defence of the Kingdom of Norway ("NMOD") and Gripen International AB ("GI"), a business unit of Saab AB (publ), hereinafter jointly referred to as the "Parties" and individually referred to as "Party". The Parties: are referring to the letter of agreement between NMOD and GI concerning industrial participation in further development of Gripen and related projects (the "LOA"); noting their wish to exchange information for the purpose of conducting evaluations of the Gripen aircraft, and pursuing discussions and work related to the Gripen Demonstrator Programme and other Gripen and defence related projects ("LOA Projects"); noting that such exchange of information may involve the disclosure of confidential information by either Party, and that the Parties wish to record their agreement to maintain the confidentiality of such information; noting that exchanging information with GI shall also include exchanging information with Saab and upon such disclosure Saab shall confirm and be bound of the terms and conditions contained herein as if Saab was a Party to the NDA; noting that exchanging information with the NMOD shall also include other Norwegian authorities and upon such disclosure said Norwegian authority shall confirm and be bound of the terms and conditions contained herein as if said Norwegian authority was a Party to the NDA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the NDA shall not be governed by any laws or regulations, which directly contradicts the introduction's premise that the NDA is made by and between parties, including the Ministry of Defence of the Kingdom of Norway, and anticipates other Norwegian authorities being bound by the terms. By negating all laws and regulations, the NDA is rendered unenforceable in the Norwegian jurisdiction that the original text and contradicted text implies, causing a strong contradiction.
